translations.en: 'Abdullah b. al-Husayn al-Tamimi came with a thousand horsemen, followed by Shabath b. Rab'i with a thousand horsemen, and Muhammad b. al-Ash'ath b. Qays al-Kindi also with a thousand horsemen. 'Umar b. Sa'd was given authority over the people and they were commanded to hear and obey him. 'Ubaydullah b. Ziyad was informed that 'Umar b. Sa'd was fraternizing with al-Husayn and was reluctant to fight him. So he dispatched Shimr b. Dhil Jawshan with four thousand horsemen and wrote: 'When this letter reaches you, do not delay with al-Husayn b. 'Ali. Seize him and cut off the water from him.' When the letter reached 'Umar b. Sa'd (may Allah curse him), he ordered his herald to announce: 'We have given al-Husayn and his companions their day and their night.' Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) stood and addressed his companions: 'O Allah! Surely I know of no household more righteous, more pure, and more clean than my household; nor companions better than my companions. You see what has befallen us. You are released from your pledge of allegiance to me. This night has enveloped you, so take it as your mount and scatter in its darkness, for indeed the people only want me.' 'Abdullah b. Muslim b. 'Aqil b. Abi Talib stood and said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! What would the people say of us if we abandoned our elder and our master without fighting alongside him?! No, by Allah! We will give our lives for your life and our blood for your blood.' A man named Zuhayr b. al-Qayn al-Bajali stood and said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! I wish I were killed and revived for you a hundred times so that Allah might defend you, the Ahl al-Bayt, through me.' Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) ordered a trench to be dug around his camp and filled with firewood. Then he sent his son 'Ali (peace be upon him) with thirty horsemen and twenty foot soldiers to fetch water. Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) began to recite [verse]: 'O Time, shame on you as a companion! How many a seeker and companion you have slain! The matter belongs to the Majestic alone. Every living soul treads my path.' Then he said to his companions: 'Stand and drink water — let it be your last provision. Perform ablution, bathe, and wash your garments, for they will be your shrouds.'
